segments.s644-g41: ورا is the clitic-like object/adpositional pronoun meaning 'for him' or 'to him' and precedes the noun phrase. دخمه‌ای is the indefinite noun 'a tomb' (دخمه + ـای for indefiniteness). ساختند is 3rd-person plural past 'they built/made'. شاهوار is a postpositive adjective 'lofty/regal' describing the tomb; in poetry adjectives may follow the verb phrase.
segments.s644-g42: ابا here introduces an adverbial phrase 'on account of / at' (archaic poetic usage) governing مرگ او 'his death' (مرگ as noun, او = 'his/he'). خلق is the subject 'the people', شد is the past 3rd-singular of شدن 'became', and سوگوار (written here سوکوار) is the predicate adjective 'mournful' describing خلق. The clause literally: 'At his death the people became mournful.'
segments.s644-g43: کنون is the temporal adverb 'now'. پرسخن is a compound adjective/nominal 'full of speech' or 'verbose' modifying the following noun. مغزم is 'my brain/mind' (مغز + ـم possessive suffix), with the possessor expressed as a clitic on the noun. اندیشه کرد is a light-verb construction: اندیشه (thought) + کرد (did) = 'took thought / reflected' (past tense).
segments.s644-g44: بگویم is a first-person present/subjunctive-like form used by the narrator as a discourse marker 'let me say' / 'I will tell'. جهان جستن is a verbal-nominal phrase 'seeking the world' (جهان 'the world' + جستن infinitive 'to seek' used here as a participial/relative phrase). یزدگرد is the proper name 'Yezdgerd' and is the logical subject of 'جهان جستن' — thus the clause is 'Yezdgerd, seeking the world', introduced by the narrator's 'let me tell'.
segments.s644-g17: گروهی که بایست کردند گرد is a relative clause: گروهی 'a number/some' + که (relative 'who/that') introduces the clause. بایست کردند is a compound past (بایست = 'stand' + کردند auxiliary) meaning 'stood', and گرد placed after the verb means 'around'. Word order: Relative pronoun + verb phrase, with the adverbial 'گرد' (around) following the verb.
segments.s644-g18: بر شاه شد پور او یزدگرد uses بر شاه ('on/over the kingship' = 'as king' or 'to the throne') as a prepositional phrase marking the role acquired; شد is past 'became'. پور او یزدگرد is an appositive noun phrase: پور او 'his son' followed by the proper name یزدگرد identifying him.
segments.s644-g19: به پیش بزرگان بدو داد تاج is a prepositional phrase + verb clause: به پیش بزرگان 'in the presence of the elders' (به + پیش = 'to/in front of'), بدو is the enclitic/old-fashioned indirect object 'to him', and داد is the past verb 'gave'. The direct object تاج follows the verb here.
segments.s644-g20: همان طوق با افسر و تخت عاج is a noun phrase describing the gift: همان 'that same', طوق 'torque/necklace (a royal collar)', followed by با + items 'with an afsar and an ivory throne'. Note افسر here is the royal headpiece/diadem sense, and تخت عاج is a noun + modifier construction 'throne (تخت) of ivory (عاج)'.
segments.s644-g21: پرستیدن ایزد آمدش رای is headed by the verbal noun/infinitive پرستیدن ایزد 'the worshipping of God' acting as the stimulus/subject; آمدش is آمد + enclitic -ش 'came to him', and رای is the noun 'counsel/plan' that 'came to him'. Grammatically this is a nominal clause saying 'the worship of God brought him counsel'.
segments.s644-g22: بینداخت تاج و بپردخت جای contains two coordinated past verbs. بینداخت (past) takes تاج as its direct object and here conveys 'threw off / cast aside' (classical sense). و links the verbs; بپردخت (past of پرداختن) plus جای 'place/position' is idiomatic: پرداختن جای can mean 'to assume/attend to the place' or 'to take up the position'. Word order is Verb + Object and then Verb + Object for the second coordinated clause.
segments.s644-g23: گرفتش ز کردار گیتی شتاب begins with گرفتَ + enclitic -ش (گرفتَش) 'seized him'. ز کردار گیتی is an ablative/causal phrase 'from/because of the conduct of the world' (i.e., 'regarding worldly affairs'), and شتاب is the noun 'haste' that functions as the thing that seized him. Order: verb with enclitic object, followed by the ablative phrase and the noun describing what afflicted him.
segments.s644-g24: چو شب تیره شد کرد آهنگ خواب is a temporal clause: چو = 'when', شب تیره شد 'the night became dark'. کرد آهنگ خواب is literal 'made an intention/turned his aim to sleep' (کرد + آهنگ = 'set his intention'), so the whole clause means 'when night grew dark he set his mind on sleep'. Note classical word order where the finite verb شد appears after the subject, and کرد + آهنگ is a light-verb + noun construction meaning 'to direct oneself toward'.
segments.s644-g1: برین سان (= بر + این (orthographic ین) + سان) is a fixed phrase meaning “in this way / thus.” همی is the classical continuous particle meaning “continually/kept on.” خورد here is literal “ate” used idiomatically with شست (“thumb”) to mean fretting/worrying (literally “he kept biting his thumb”). سال follows to indicate the duration “three years.” Word order is idiomatic: manner + continuous particle + verb + object + duration.
segments.s644-g2: کس اندر زمانه نبودش همال is a simple negative clause: کس here functions as “no one,” اندر زمانه “in the age/realm,” نبودش is the past negative ‘was not (for him)’ and همال is the noun ‘porter/carrier.’ The clause means “there was no porter in the land (for him).” Note the enclitic -ش on نبودش referring back to the person concerned.
segments.s644-g3: سر سال is an idiom meaning “at the beginning/top of the year” (i.e., at new-year time). در پیش او شد دبیر literally “a scribe came/appeared before him”: شد is the verb “happened/appeared,” and the phrase places the scribe (دبیر) into the prepositional phrase در پیش او (in his presence). Classical word order puts شد after the locative phrase.
segments.s644-g4: خردمند موبد is a nominal phrase “a wise mōbed.” که introduces a relative clause “who …” and بودش وزیر is “was his vizier” where بودش (بود + enclitic -ش) is the past copula referring back to the king (his), and وزیر is the predicate noun. The relative clause modifies موبد.
segments.s644-g5: که شد at the start introduces a circumstance “when/that it became.” گنج شاه is the genitive ‘the king’s treasury.’ بزرگان تهی describes the result — ‘the great ones (the nobles) were left empty/without (wealth).’ The clause as a whole means that the royal treasury was depleted and the nobles were (thereby) impoverished.
segments.s644-g6: کنون آمدم is first‑person past ‘I have come (now).’ تا introduces purpose (“in order that / to”), and the subordinate clause چه فرمان دهی uses the present/jussive verb دهی (2nd person) after تا to ask “what command you will give.” Together: “Now I have come to ask what order you will give.”
segments.s644-g7: هرانکس که is a free relative ‘whoever.’ دارد is the present indicative ‘has/possesses.’ روانش (روان + enclitic -ش) ‘his soul/mind’ and خرد ‘wisdom’ form the object: “whoever possesses wisdom in his soul.” Note the possessive enclitic on روان.
segments.s644-g8: به مال کسان is “toward/with regard to other people’s property” (به introducing the object), and از ... ننگرد is a negative present verb form meaning “does not look/does not turn to / does not covet.” Together the segment says that a wise person does not look to (i.e., covet) the possessions of others. The negative verb ننگرد is classical present/imperative-type form.
segments.s644-g33: چنین بود = 'it was thus/so' (چنین + بود). تا بود uses تا ('until/while') with بود to mean 'while it lasted' or 'as long as it was'. و این بود روز inverts normal order: این بود روز = 'this was (the) day' (بود followed by the noun روز for emphasis). The repeated بود forms are simple past/copular uses typical of poetic diction.
segments.s644-g34: تو دل را به آز و فزونی مسوز = direct address (تو) + object-marked دل را ('your heart' / 'the heart') placed before the verb for emphasis. به + nouns (به آز و فزونی) indicates 'with/through/by (greed and excess)'. مسوز is the negative imperative of سوزاندن ('do not burn' / 'do not torment'): short, archaic/poetic negative imperative used here.
segments.s644-g35: بترسد is the classical/persian present (b- prefix + present stem) used as indicative/subjunctive meaning 'fears' or 'will fear' in poetic narrative. The subject follows the verb: دل سنگ و آهن = 'a heart of stone and iron'. ز مرگ = 'from/of death' (ز is the poetic form of از).
segments.s644-g36: هم ایدر ترا ساختن نیست برگ is ordered in a compressed, archaic way: هم ایدر = 'even/also here'; ترا is the 2nd-person clitic (for 'you' / 'for you') attached in the sequence; ساختن نیست = 'there is no making/being made' (ساختن as infinitive/noun + نیست = 'is not'); برگ = 'leaf/page' (object placed at end). Literal: 'even here for-you making is not a leaf' → 'even here no leaf is made for you.'
segments.s644-g37: بی‌آزاری و مردمی بایدت = two coordinated qualities (بی‌آزاری 'harmlessness' and مردمی 'being of the people/affable') followed by بایدت. بایدت is باید + enclitic -ت, meaning 'it is necessary for you' / 'you must'. Word order puts the required qualities before the modal necessity.
segments.s644-g38: گذشته چو خواهی که نگزایدت is a conditional/subjunctive clause: گذشته = 'the past' or 'what has passed'; چو = 'if/when' (introducing the condition); خواهی = second-person present 'you want'; که introduces the subordinate clause; نگزایدت is negative with enclitic -ت, meaning 'it will not harm you' (نگزاید + -ت). Together: 'so that the past, if you wish, will not harm you.'
segments.s644-g39: همی نو کنم بخشش و داد اوی = همی (continuous/always) + نو (new) + کنم (first-person present/subjunctive 'I do/make') giving 'I will continually make new/renew'. بخشش و داد اوی = 'his forgiveness and justice' with اوی as an archaic possessive/pronoun form ('his'). The verb comes after the adverbial phrase, a normal poetic order.
segments.s644-g40: مبادا که گیرد به بد یاد اوی = مبادا ('may it not be' / 'lest') introduces a negative wish; که introduces the clause; گیرد is present/subjunctive 'take/seize' (here 'take into memory'); به + بد + یاد = 'into bad remembrance' (به marks the state or direction); اوی = 'his' (possessive). Whole clause: 'lest he be taken into bad remembrance' / 'may he not be remembered ill.'
segments.s644-g25: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. بنمود is a past tense verb meaning 'appeared/revealed' placed before its object (a common poetic order). دست آفتاب is a metaphoric noun phrase ('hand of the sun' = sunray/appearance) and از نشیب is a prepositional phrase ('from the descent/slope') marking source or place.
segments.s644-g26: This clause names the affected parties then gives the result: دل (heart) + موبد (mōbed) and شاه (king) are coordinated as the subjects whose state changes. شد is the past-tense copula 'became'. پر is the predicative adjective 'full (of)' and نهیب is the complement noun ('a cry/rebuke' here rendered as 'thunderous alarm'); the whole means their hearts became filled with that quality.
segments.s644-g27: که introduces a clause of explanation/reason ('for' / 'that'). شاه جهان is a noun phrase 'king of the world'. برنخیرد is an older/poetic verbal form meaning roughly 'will not refrain/cease to act' (here a negative sense implied in context), and همی is an archaic progressive/emphatic particle marking continued or general action.
segments.s644-g28: مگر introduces an exception clause ('unless'). از کرانی is a prepositional phrase 'from/out of the bounds' (کرانی = border/boundary). گریزد is a present/habitual-form verb 'flees/escapes' (classical Persian uses this form for potential actions), and the final همی again gives a continuous or emphatic shade to the verb.
segments.s644-g29: بیامد is simple past 'came'. به نزد is a prepositional phrase meaning 'to the presence of / to the court of', followed by پدر یزدگرد 'father Yezdegerd' as the addressee/location.
segments.s644-g30: چو دیدش is a temporal clause 'when he saw him' with the object clitic -ش attached to دید. کف اندر دهانش is a locative/nominal phrase 'the kaf/palm/omen in his mouth' (کف here is the item observed), and فسرد is a past tense verb 'interpreted / explained'. The verb applies to that phrase: upon seeing him, he interpreted that sign.
segments.s644-g31: ورا is an old pronominal form 'to him / him' used as the object of دید 'saw'. پژمرده رنگ رخان is a compact adjectival phrase: پژمرده 'withered/paled' + رنگ 'complexion/appearance' + رخان 'loose/relaxed' describing a wan, slack appearance; the adjectives modify the person seen.
segments.s644-g32: به + دیبای زربفت is a prepositional phrase 'in/over a gold-brocaded robe' (دیبا = brocade, زربفت = gold-woven). بر داده is a past participial/poetic sequence 'having been given/upon' and جان 'life/soul' is the affected noun; together this compressed poetic phrasing conveys 'with his life given over / his life laid upon (the brocade)', i.e. a helpless, near-death image.
segments.s644-g9: چنین پاسخ آورد = “(he) brought/gave such an answer.” چینیِی (چنین) is an adverb/demonstrative 'thus' placed before the noun; پاسخ is the noun 'answer' and آورد is a simple past verb 'brought/said' (classical past). این خود is emphatic: این + خود = 'this very one / he himself' (the demonstrative + reflexive/emphatic). مساز is a prohibitive/negative imperative (classical m- prefix + ساز) here functioning as the opening of the quoted speech: 'do not make/assume/think.'
segments.s644-g10: که introduces the content clause ('that...'). هستیم is 1st‑person plural present 'we are' (predicate of the clause). زین is an archaic/prepositional element here meaning 'of/from this' (classical contraction of از + این) and is not represented in the given senses; نقشِ ساختن is a noun/gerund 'this making/this construction' (ساختن used as the object of being free from), and بی‌نیاز is an adjective 'free/exempt' modifying the subject 'we' (i.e. 'we are free/exempt of/from this making').
segments.s644-g11: جهان را places 'the world' as a topical/definite object with the postpositive را. ب + دان here is an archaic/compounded particle meaning 'at that / thereby' (b- as a prefix and دان/آن element), and باز is an adverb 'again.' هل is anomalous in the provided lexical senses (it appears here as a small particle or interjection in the line and not as the literal 'cardamom'); کافرید is a 2nd‑person plural predicate 'you are unbelievers' (کافر + ‑ید). The phrase addresses or reproaches 'you unbelievers' in connection with the following verb 'saw.'
segments.s644-g12: سر گردش آفرینش is a nominal phrase: سر 'head/top' governed by گردش 'turn/rotation' with آفرینش 'of creation' (a genitive/possessive relation: 'the head/top of the rotation of creation'). بدید is a past verb 'saw' (classical past form) and links to the preceding address: '(you) saw.'
segments.s644-g13: همی is an adverb 'still/ever/indeed' giving continuity or emphasis. بگذرد is a present/subjunctive form used impersonally or as a future-like 'will pass/return' (classical usage). چرخ و یزدان are the coordinated subjects 'the wheel (fortune) and God' and به جای is a prepositional phrase 'to/to their place' or 'to the rightful place.' Together: 'the wheel and God will pass/return to place.'
segments.s644-g14: به نیکی is a directional phrase 'toward goodness' or 'to goodness.' ترا و مرا are the objects 'you' and 'me' marked by the enclitic object marker (here tokenized as ت + را and م + را); these are the persons to be guided. رهنمای is an optative/jussive form (third person imperative/verb-like noun) meaning 'may (it/they) be a guide' or 'let (them) guide' — i.e. 'may (the wheel and God) guide you and me to goodness.' Note the enclitic pronouns appear split in the tokenization but function as standard object pronouns.
segments.s644-g15: بخفت is a past tense verb 'he slept' (simple past). آن شب is a temporal adverbial 'that night.' و links the two time expressions; بامداد پگاه is a tautological/poetic doubling meaning 'at dawn/early morning' (both words mark the morning time).
segments.s644-g16: بیامد is a past tense 'came.' به درگاه is the prepositional phrase 'to the court/royal threshold' (درگاه as 'royal court/presence'). بی‌مر is an obscure/archaic epithet in the manuscript (the supplied lemma list marks it uncertain), and it modifies سپاه 'the host/army.' Thus: 'the host came to the (said) court.'

sentences.s644-s21: They built for him a lofty tomb; at his death the people became mournful.
sentences.s644-s22: Now my speech-filled mind took thought; let me tell of Yezdgerd seeking the world.
sentences.s644-s9: A number of men stood around the king; his son Yazdegerd became king.
sentences.s644-s10: In the presence of the elders they gave him the crown — that same torque with an afsar and an ivory throne.
sentences.s644-s11: Worship of God brought him counsel; he cast off the crown and took his place.
sentences.s644-s12: Haste seized him from worldly affairs; when the night became dark he turned his attention to sleep.
sentences.s644-s1: He fretted like this for three years, and there was no porter in the land.
sentences.s644-s2: At the new year a scribe came before him — a wise mōbed who was his vizier.
sentences.s644-s3: When the king’s treasury was emptied and the great men were impoverished, I have now come to ask what decree you will give.
sentences.s644-s4: Whoever has wisdom in his soul will not covet other people’s property.
sentences.s644-s17: Thus it was while it lasted, and this was your day; do not burn your heart with greed and excess.
sentences.s644-s18: Even a heart of stone and iron fears death; here, too, no leaf is made for you.
sentences.s644-s19: Be harmless and affable to the people; let the past be such that it will not harm you when you wish.
sentences.s644-s20: I will always renew his forgiveness and justice; may it not be that he is taken into bad remembrance.
sentences.s644-s13: When the sun's hand appeared from the slope, the mōbed's heart and the king's were filled with a sudden, thunderous alarm.
sentences.s644-s14: For the king of the world will not refrain; only if he flees beyond the borders might he escape.
sentences.s644-s15: He came to his father Yezdegerd; when he saw him, he interpreted the 'kaf' inside his mouth.
sentences.s644-s16: He saw him pale and faded in complexion, with loosened features; wearing a gold-brocaded robe, his life seemed given over.
sentences.s644-s5: He himself answered thus: "Do not suppose that we are exempt from this making."
sentences.s644-s6: At that, you unbelievers again looked upon the world and saw the turning head of creation.
sentences.s644-s7: The wheel and God will yet return to their place; may they guide you and me to goodness.
sentences.s644-s8: He slept that night, and at dawn the host came to the court (بی‌مر).
